input.name: Fix button contrast
input.overview: Status buttons and marks still use dark-theme pastel text hexes on the light Warm Horizon palette, so labels wash out everywhere. Fix semantic tokens (especially `--danger`), then replace those pastels with dark token colors on soft tints so every page shares one readable, color-coded system.
input.plan:
# Fix low-contrast status buttons and marks

## Root cause

The panel is a **light** theme ([`relocation_jobs/static/design-tokens.css`](relocation_jobs/static/design-tokens.css) — Warm Horizon / paper `#fcfaf7`), but many status controls in [`relocation_jobs/static/styles.css`](relocation_jobs/static/styles.css) still use **dark-theme pastel text** (`#6ee7b7`, `#a7f3d0`, `#fca5a5`, `#8eb8ff`, `#94a3b8`, `#5eead4`, `#fbbf24`, `#c4b5fd`, …). Soft backgrounds + pastel labels = unreadable.

Two compounding issues:

1. **Hardcoded pastel `color:`** on action buttons / badges / hover states (hover often *lightens* text further — wrong for light UI).
2. **`--danger` is wrong:** it aliases `--color-rule` (`#a9c1d1`, sky rule), so danger soft fills are not a real red. Rejected/remove styles then fight with hardcoded pink pastels.

Board list also has a late override block (`.company-card .applied-btn` etc. ~L7151) that remaps several controls to orange/navy fills. Detail views often sit outside that override, so the pale base styles show through — which matches the screenshot.

## Approach

Keep soft tinted backgrounds; use **dark semantic text** (`var(--success)`, `var(--warn)`, fixed `var(--danger)`, `var(--referral)` / `var(--accent-blue)`, `var(--muted)` / `var(--color-ink)`). Hover/active keep the same hue family, never lighter pastels.

No JS/HTML changes — CSS tokens + class styles only.

## 1. Fix semantic tokens

In [`relocation_jobs/static/design-tokens.css`](relocation_jobs/static/design-tokens.css):

- Set `--danger` to a readable red (e.g. `oklch(48% 0.14 25)`), not `--color-rule`.
- Keep `--danger-soft` as `color-mix` of that danger into paper.
- Leave `--success` / `--warn` as-is (already mid-dark and suitable on soft fills).

## 2. Sweep pastel text in `styles.css`

Replace dark-theme hex `color:` (and matching hover/active that go *lighter*) for these families:

| Family | Selectors (representative) | Text becomes |
|--------|----------------------------|--------------|
| Success / applied | `.applied-btn`, `.restore-job-btn`, `.fetch-ok-btn:hover` | `var(--success)` |
| Danger / rejected | `.rejected-btn`, `.remove-company-btn`, `.show-rejected-btn`, `.rejected-jobs-heading`, `.company-more-menu .remove-company-btn` | `var(--danger)` |
| Referral / blue | `.referral-btn`, `.badge.referral:hover`, `.fetch-company-btn:hover`, `.reapply-btn:hover` | `var(--referral)` / `var(--accent-blue)` / `var(--color-ink)` |
| Seen / slate | `.saw-before-btn`, `.hide-reason-trigger--expired`, `.badge.expired` | `var(--muted)` or `var(--color-ink-2)` |
| Warn / not-for-me | `.show-not-for-me-btn` hover/active, `.badge.not-for-me`, `.pin-job-btn.is-pinned` | `var(--warn)` (darker amber than `#fbbf24`) |
| Teal / wrong-location | `.hide-reason-trigger--wrong-location`, `.badge.wrong-location`, option dots | dark teal via token or `oklch` ~40% L (not `#5eead4`) |
| Awaiting / purple | `.awaiting-response-btn` | `var(--accent-purple)` / `var(--color-ink)` |
| Seen titles | `.position-card.position-seen .job-title` | `var(--color-ink-2)` (company-card already does this; make base match) |
| Pagination | `.filter-btn.board-page-num.is-active` | `var(--accent-blue)` instead of `color-mix(... 65%, white)` |

Also fix badge variants that still use pastel text: `.badge.not-for-me`, `.badge.wrong-location`, `.badge.expired`, `.badge.referral:hover`.

Leave already-readable pieces alone (e.g. `.looking-to-apply-btn` using `var(--accent-blue)`, `.company-position-badge--*` teal/amber hexes, `.badge.cv-pdf`).

## 3. Align `.company-card` status overrides

Update the Hum remap block (~L7151–7193) so it does **not** collapse distinct statuses into orange/navy fills. Prefer the same soft-tint + dark-label system as the base rules (or remove the status-button remaps and keep only structural card chrome). Goal: board and detail pages look consistent and status hues stay recognizable.

## 4. Verify

Hard-refresh the panel and spot-check:

- Company board position actions (Mark applied / Interested / Seen / Referral / Not for me / Rejected)
- Company detail / CV workspace (same buttons + PDF/Queue badges)
- Hide-reason trigger + popover option dots
- Company remove / fetch-ok hover
- Rejected / not-for-me section headings and badges

No pytest needed (CSS-only).

Spot-check board + detail + hide-reason + badges for contrast and distinct status hues

content.text: Browser MCP isn't available — verifying contrast with a quick ratio check against the new token colors.

input.command:
python3 <<'PY'
# Contrast check: soft tint bg + dark semantic text (approx sRGB)
# WCAG relative luminance

def hex_to_rgb(h):
    h=h.lstrip('#')
    return tuple(int(h[i:i+2],16)/255 for i in (0,2,4))

def lin(c):
    return c/12.92 if c<=0.04045 else ((c+0.055)/1.055)**2.4

def lum(rgb):
    r,g,b=rgb
    return 0.2126*lin(r)+0.7152*lin(g)+0.0722*lin(b)

def contrast(fg,bg):
    L1,L2=lum(fg),lum(bg)
    hi,lo=max(L1,L2),min(L1,L2)
    return (hi+0.05)/(lo+0.05)

def mix(fg, bg, pct):
    # approx srgb mix
    return tuple(fg[i]*pct + bg[i]*(1-pct) for i in range(3))

# Approximate oklch -> use known equivalents from design
paper = hex_to_rgb('fcfaf7')
# success oklch(42% 0.09 145) ~ dark green; approx #2d6b45
success = hex_to_rgb('2d6b45')
# warn oklch(55% 0.14 55) ~ #b06a12-ish
warn = hex_to_rgb('b06a12')
# danger oklch(48% 0.14 25) ~ #b53a3a-ish  
danger = hex_to_rgb('b53a3a')
referral = hex_to_rgb('345c7c')  # accent-blue / ink-2
ink = hex_to_rgb('0e3a69')
muted = hex_to_rgb('5d7488')
ink2 = hex_to_rgb('345c7c')
teal = hex_to_rgb('1f6b66')  # oklch(40% 0.08 180) approx

pairs = [
    ('applied', success, mix(success, paper, 0.14)),
    ('rejected', danger, mix(danger, paper, 0.14)),
    ('referral', referral, mix(referral, paper, 0.12)),
    ('warn', warn, mix(warn, paper, 0.14)),
    ('seen', ink2, mix(muted, paper, 0.08)),
    ('expired', ink2, mix(muted, paper, 0.10)),
    ('wrong-loc', teal, mix(teal, paper, 0.10)),
    ('old-applied-pastel', hex_to_rgb('6ee7b7'), mix(success, paper, 0.14)),
]
print(f"{'label':22} {'ratio':>6} AA?")
for name,fg,bg in pairs:
    r=contrast(fg,bg)
    print(f"{name:22} {r:6.2f}  {'PASS' if r>=4.5 else 'fail' if r<3 else 'AA-large'}")
PY

# Confirm CSS served and danger token present

curl -s -o /dev/null -w "panel %{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1:5051/

content.text:
Status marks and buttons should be readable again across the panel.

**Cause:** Light Warm Horizon theme still had dark-theme pastel label colors (`#6ee7b7`, `#fca5a5`, etc.), so text disappeared on soft fills. `--danger` was also aliased to the sky rule color.

**Changes:**
- [`design-tokens.css`](relocation_jobs/static/design-tokens.css) — real red `--danger` / `--danger-soft` (homepage tokens kept in lockstep)
- [`styles.css`](relocation_jobs/static/styles.css) — status buttons/badges use dark semantic tokens; hovers no longer lighten text
- Removed `.company-card` remaps that forced orange/navy fills so board and detail share the same color-coded system

Hard-refresh the panel to pick up the CSS.

input.new_string:
/** Lift this card and its company above siblings while a popover is open
   *  so the popover is not clipped behind later position/company cards. */
input.old_string:
/** Lift this card and its company above siblings while a popover is open.
   *  Dimmed cards (.position-seen use opacity < 1) create a stacking context
   *  that traps the popover's z-index behind later siblings. We target the
   *  inner .position-card (which carries the opacity SC) so the SC itself
   *  gets positioned above siblings.  We also raise the .company-card so
   *  the popover doesn't fall behind the *next* company card. */

content.text:
Seen jobs no longer fade the whole card.

They now read like a visited Google result: **muted, lighter-weight title** on a full-opacity card. Hover brings the title back toward navy.
